open_findings.title: Nav label “Activity” vs page headline “Job queue”
open_findings.description: Sidebar nav shows “Activity” (Shell.tsx NAV_ITEMS) while the page eyebrow also says Activity but the H1 reads “Job queue”. Not wrong, but users may wonder if they are the same section; consider aligning headline with nav or adding a short subline.

proof_hooks.summary: apps/dashboard/components/Shell.tsx:89-93 — nav label "Activity" for jobs view.

history.notes: Terminology drift within single view.
Normalized proof_hooks from agent hooks[] field.

open_findings.title: Project page loads the full portfolio via /api/projects
open_findings.description: ProjectPageClient uses usePortfolioProjects, which always GETs /api/projects and filters client-side to the current project. For users with many projects this duplicates work and payload size versus a single-project GET.

suggested_fix.approach: Add GET /api/projects/[name] as the primary load for the project route (or hydrate from server component) and keep list fetch for the portfolio index only.
steps: Compare existing /api/projects/[name] behavior with ProjectPageClient data needs.
Reduce redundant refetches after navigation between projects.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.

open_findings.title: repair_service migration DROP POLICY names may not match earlier migration
open_findings.description: 20260402231116_repair_service.sql drops policies named users_can_view_repair_jobs, etc. The earlier 20260402163740_rls_policies.sql creates policies named "Users can access repair jobs for their projects". IF EXISTS avoids errors, but the older FOR ALL policy may remain alongside newer SELECT/INSERT policies, producing redundant or confusing policy sets until manually reconciled.

proof_hooks.summary: DROP uses different names than CREATE in 002
proof_hooks.value: DROP POLICY IF EXISTS "users_can_view_repair_jobs" vs CREATE POLICY "Users can access repair jobs for their projects"

suggested_fix.approach: In a follow-up migration, list policies on repair_jobs in production and drop obsolete ones explicitly.
steps: SELECT polname FROM pg_policy JOIN pg_class ... WHERE relname = 'repair_jobs';
DROP redundant policies; document intended final set.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.

open_findings.title: FKs removed from penny_audit_* and related tables for async sync
open_findings.description: 20260403110028_fix_audit_table_schemas.sql drops foreign keys from penny_audit_jobs, penny_audit_runs, penny_orchestration_events, and penny_project_snapshots to penny_projects so jobs can exist before the project row. This trades referential integrity for pipeline flexibility; orphan rows and typos in project_name are possible.

suggested_fix.approach: Periodic cleanup job or CHECK using validated project_name from application layer; optional deferred FK via materialized project registry.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.
Status synced from Linear (PLP-601): Todo -> accepted

open_findings.title: Product name casing is inconsistent (Penny vs penny)
open_findings.description: README and repo branding use “Penny”; root metadata title and in-app strings use lowercase “penny” (e.g. layout metadata, document.title suffixes, footer). Decide a single public-facing rule (sentence case in prose, lowercase wordmark in UI, etc.) and align metadata, titles, and marketing copy.

proof_hooks.summary: README.md title "Penny v3.0" vs dashboard metadata.

history.notes: No prior open_findings entry; branding question for default voice.
Normalized proof_hooks from agent hooks[] field.
Decision: Penny

open_findings.title: Broad GRANT on repair_jobs to anon and authenticated
open_findings.description: Migration grants SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON repair_jobs TO anon, authenticated. RLS still applies, but widening table privileges increases blast radius if policies are misconfigured or bypassed. Confirm this matches intended Supabase API surface.

proof_hooks.value: GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE ON repair_jobs TO anon, authenticated;

suggested_fix.approach: If only Edge Functions and server should mutate repair_jobs, prefer narrower GRANTs plus strict RLS tests.
steps: Review product requirement for client-side repair job creation.
Add automated policy tests or integration tests for anon/authenticated.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from data id field.
Decision: Narrow GRANTs

open_findings.title: Deployment target ambiguity: Netlify config coexists with Vercel project file
open_findings.description: `netlify.toml` defines the dashboard build (`turbo run build --filter penny-dashboard`) and Next plugin, while `apps/dashboard/vercel.json` specifies framework, output directory, and npm-based install/build. It is unclear which platform is authoritative for production, which affects env var naming, build commands, and review of deployment safety.

suggested_fix.approach: Document the single source of truth for dashboard hosting; remove or archive the unused platform file to avoid drift.
steps: Decide Netlify vs Vercel for dashboard
Align lockfile/install commands with pnpm workspace

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from deploy id field.
Decision: Netlify is where Penny will be deployed

open_findings.title: Should findings have a composite index on (project_id, status, created_at)?
open_findings.description: Migrations define idx_findings_project and idx_findings_status separately. Dashboard queries often filter by project_id and optionally status with ordering by created_at. A composite index may reduce heap fetches versus BitmapAnd of two indexes, but this should be validated with real row counts and plans.

proof_hooks.value: CREATE INDEX idx_findings_project ON findings(project_id); CREATE INDEX idx_findings_status ON findings(status);
EXPLAIN (ANALYZE, BUFFERS) SELECT id, title FROM findings WHERE project_id = $1 AND status = $2 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50;

suggested_fix.approach: Run EXPLAIN ANALYZE on production-like data; add CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on (project_id, status, created_at DESC) if seq scans or high buffer reads appear.
steps: Capture baseline plan from Supabase SQL editor.
Compare before/after index addition in a staging branch.

history.notes: Normalized finding_id from perf agent id field; merged history.
Decision: Add index

open_findings.title: readAuditRunFiles ignores nested audits/runs/<date>/*.json layout
open_findings.description: WORKFLOW stores agent outputs under audits/runs/YYYY-MM-DD/<run_id>.json. readAuditRunFiles only readdirSync files ending in .json at the top level of audits/runs, so nested run files are never loaded into getEngineStatus / last_audit_date aggregation.

proof_hooks.summary: audits/runs/2026-04-07/*.json exist only under date folders, not at runs root.

suggested_fix.approach: Recursively walk audits/runs or rely on audits/index.json for run metadata only.

history.notes: Promoted from data-20260409-221501.

open_findings.title: Onboarding cluster POST returns artifacts but does not persist them on the server
open_findings.description: POST /api/projects/[name]/onboarding/cluster collects cluster-specific artifacts (git history, manifests, CSS tokens) and returns them in JSON. The handler documents that a full implementation would save into the Project model; without that, only the client retains the payload across navigation unless it re-fetches or stores locally.

proof_hooks.summary: Handler comment: save to project.clusterArtifacts deferred; response-only today
Success response returns artifacts without repo.update persistence
